text:
The hammer throw, shot put and discus are amongst the ultimate displays of strength at theParis2024Olympics.

The shot put and discus have been part of the Olympic schedule since the very first athletics programme in 1896.

The hammer throw, meanwhile, has featured at the greatest show on Earth since 1900.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 games

But exactly how much do the hammer, shot and discus weigh? Here is everything you need to know.

The hammer thrown by male competitors weighs 7.26kg, whilst the hammer thrown by female athletes is 4kg. This incorporates the metal ball, wire and grip.

The maximum size for men is 100mm and 85mm for women. The wire attached to the weight must not be longer than 1.22m, which is the same for both men and women.

Canada’s Ethan Katzberg has claimed gold in the men’s hammer throw in Paris, ahead of Hungary’s Bence Halász and Ukraine’s Mykhaylo Kokhan.

The women’s final is set for Tuesday.

The men’s shot weight is 7.26kg and the women’s shot weight is 4kg.

The shot, typically made out of common metals such as iron, steel and brass, is the same weight at all World Athletics events, but varies in other competitions such as school, and U20 events.

Ryan Crouser claimed gold for the USA in the men’s shot put, with compatriot Joe Kovacs in second and Jamaica’s Rajindra Campbell.

The women’s shot put is scheduled for Friday.

The discus weighs 2kg for men and 1kg for women, and athletes have a 2.5m diameter circle they must use to generate rotational energy to propel the object forwards.

The men’s final is set for Wednesday with the women’s final to take place on Monday.

title: How much the hammer throw, shot put and discus weigh at the Olympics | Metro News
description: Find out how much the hammer throw, shot put and discus weighs at the Paris 2024 Olympics, with athletes eyeing gold at the Stade de France.
